body_txt: In Chinese traditions, the center is also included. While not a direction itself, it is important due to the fact that directions are only meaningful in relation to a specific location (basic geometry). Frequently, this will be the hero's hometown, but if not, it is generally where the narrative begins. East and west are the same all over the world, but north and south are opposites, depending on which hemisphere you're in. In the northern hemisphere, north is cold and south is warm , while in the southern hemisphere it's reversed. However, since most of the inhabitable land is in the northern hemisphere, the representation of north as colder tends to hold sway. See also The Four Gods , where each of them is associated with a particular cardinal direction, as well as the sub-index Polar Tropes , which focuses on tropes associated with the North and South Poles.
